title: ByteBuffer from component iteration must keep unsafe native memory alive
category: use-after-free: native memory freed while a derived ByteBuffer still references it (Java ↔ native/Unsafe)
crash_site: JVM SIGSEGV / corrupted data reading a java.nio.ByteBuffer obtained from a Buffer component via forEachReadable/forEachWritable. The ByteBuffer access is ordinary; the native memory behind it was already released.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the ByteBuffer access site where it crashes, and ReadableComponent / UnsafeBuffer.readableBuffer which hand out the buffer. The graded defect is directBuffer creating a ByteBuffer over native memory with nothing keeping that memory alive.
ground_truth.fix_summary: forEachReadable/forEachWritable on an UnsafeBuffer could hand out a java.nio.ByteBuffer backed by native memory; if that ByteBuffer was siphoned out of the iteration and outlived the UnsafeBuffer, nothing kept the UnsafeMemory alive -> UAF. Fix: PlatformDependent.directBuffer takes an attachment object (the owning UnsafeMemory) so GC keeps the native memory alive while the ByteBuffer is reachable; readableBuffer/writableBuffer pass it.

admission.note: Authored from PR #12036 body+diff. crash != cause: SIGSEGV in plain ByteBuffer access; cause is directBuffer creating a ByteBuffer over native memory with no attachment to keep it alive once siphoned out of forEachReadable. MULTI-FILE (6 files) -> grading accepts directBuffer OR readableBuffer/writableBuffer + the keep-alive mechanism. Java <-> native Unsafe memory. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/netty/netty/pull/12036 — ByteBuffer from component iteration must keep unsafe memory alive; fix 71d829a34236, base 5b0e0a9820f1.
